titleStmt.title: Letter from John Sweetman to Sir John Griffith, 23 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Sweetman

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A request made by John Sweetman (1844-1936) to Sir John Purser Griffith (1848-1938). Sweetman's request was made on behalf of political activist and editor of 'The Irishman', Herbert Moore Pim (1883-1950). Sweetman had written an article which is due to appear in a forthcoming edition of 'The Irishman' in which he mentioned a rumour that Sir John Griffith's son resigned his position as engineer-in-chief of the Dublin Port and Docks board. He requests that Sir John Griffith contact Pim on the subject.John Sweetman was a member of Sinn Féin and patron to Arthur Griffith. In 1915 he spoke out against conscription and was arrested in the aftermath of the 1916 Rising. Sir John Griffith was the chief engineer at the Dublin Port and Dock Board but took early retirement in 1912. He was knighted in 1911 and later became a member of Seanad Eireann.

msIdentifier.collection: Sweetman Family Papers, Collection List No. 156, Ms. 47,589 /12

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
DRUMBARAGH, KELLS, CO. MEATH. October 23RD 1916 Sir John P. Brosnan Griffith Rathmines Castle Dear Sir In a letter which will appear in
'The Irishman' of the 28th I have drawn attention
to the Dublin Port &Docks Board, &
mentioned a rumour I had heard that your
son had resigned his position as engineer
-in-chief to that Board owing to its recent
action. I know little of the facts of the case,
& J McHerbert M. Pim, the editor of the
Irishman, whose pen-name is A.Newman,
has asked me to see whether you would
write to him on the subject. Yours truly John Sweetman
